quotes.content: The work required trained men, and the presence of trained men - in a process with which we are familiar - made more work for more trained men.
quotes.from: 'The Making of the Middle Ages', R. W. Southern

quotes.content: "If you are going to shoot me," the lieutenant-colonel said, "please shoot me at once without further questioning. The questioning is stupid."

Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith? Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed? [...] Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
Perception of a dharma, perception of no dharma / hands open then they close / floating clouds reveal blue sky / for a thousand miles Heaven looks the same

Frustrated by his inability to make the bakufu see reason, Go-Daigo immediately began a program of invoking the assistance of the Buddhas and laying curses upon the bakufu in order to hasten its demise. Go-Daigo's personal participation in the prayers, rituals, and exorcistic magic accompanying this strategy was regarded with some misgiving by the bakufu, since it was highly unusual for a reigning emperor to take part as an invocator in any Buddhist rites. Equally troubling was the fact that esoteric practices were usually regarded as both powerful and efficacious; and even though they were conducted under the guise of prayers for the safe delivery of the pregnant Reiseimon-in, by uncanny coincidence they included "dual use" invocations and ceremonies that could also call for the destruction of enemies and had actually been used by the bakufu against the Mongols. Still, the program was to continue for at least four years until the bakufu, rightfully dubious that any birth could be so overdue, ordered the prayers stopped and arrested some of the religious figures involved.
quotes.from: 'Kenmu: Go Daigo's Revolution', Andrew Edmund Goble
